name: swipeable-row-actions
title: Swipeable Row Actions
author: Marek Rozmus
description: Reveals caller-supplied leading and trailing actions from a measured, touch-friendly row.

changes: Ported the runtime export entry mechanically from JavaScript to TypeScript and added a client boundary.
Re-exported the prop types supplied by the TypeScript component ports.
Ported PropTypes to strict TypeScript and replaced clsx with a local class join while retaining the upstream list defaults, Type values, child injection, and Tag API.
Added a local render-component type bridge for React 19 polymorphic JSX without changing runtime behavior.
Ported PropTypes and the class state to strict TypeScript and replaced clsx with a local class join without replacing the upstream drag, threshold, action, return, or removal state machine.
Handled touchcancel, pointercancel, window blur, and release outside the row; centralized global listener, animation listener, ResizeObserver, and animation-frame cleanup.
Added visible semantic reveal buttons, Escape and direction-aware arrow handling, deterministic action focus and return, inert hidden actions, and a polite destructive-result status.
Mapped leading and trailing sides, offsets, thresholds, return transforms, and destructive travel from computed RTL direction while retaining physical swipe callback values.
Re-measured the host and action content through ResizeObserver using the same single scheduled animation frame as position work, and bypassed MS animation listeners when reduced motion is requested.
Replaced physical action positioning with logical insets and added RTL-aware MS keyframes and transform origins.
Added pan-y touch behavior, visible 44-pixel reveal controls with focus indicators, and visually hidden live-status styling.
Added a reduced-motion branch that removes return, removal, width, and scale transitions or animations while retaining their final states.
Ported PropTypes to strict TypeScript and replaced clsx with a local class join while retaining the upstream action scheduling and full-swipe registration flow.
Changed the default Tag from span to a type=button action, supplied Enter and Space semantics for custom non-button tags, and kept actions out of tab order until revealed.
Tracked and cleared actionDelay and destructiveCallbackDelay timers and unregistered stale full-swipe callbacks on cleanup.
Added a local render-component type bridge for React 19 polymorphic JSX without changing runtime behavior.
Reset the new native button surface, preserved inherited caller styling, added a visible focus indicator and 44-pixel minimum target, and applied alignment to both direct and nested action content.
Disabled the full-swipe width transition when reduced motion is requested.
Ported the upstream child cloning helper mechanically to strict TypeScript.
Ported the upstream child cloning helper mechanically to strict TypeScript.
Aligned the upstream declarations with the strict TypeScript ports, symbol-valued Type API, native button default, ElementType tags, action delay, class names, and new reveal-label props.

license.scope: src/index.js, src/SwipeableList.js, src/SwipeableList.css, src/SwipeableListItem.js, src/SwipeableListItem.css, src/SwipeAction.js, src/SwipeAction.css, src/LeadingActions.js, src/LeadingActions.css, src/TrailingActions.js, src/TrailingActions.css, and src/module.d.ts

modifications.change: Ported the runtime entry to TypeScript, added the client boundary, and exposed the ported prop types.
modifications.reason: Install the hook and lifecycle based source safely in React Server Component hosts without shipping a parallel PropTypes runtime.

modifications.change: Mechanically ported the upstream list and Type contract from PropTypes and clsx to strict TypeScript and a local class join. Added a local render-component type bridge for React 19 polymorphic JSX without changing runtime behavior.
modifications.reason: Use host React without adding the upstream project's undeclared prop-types or clsx development dependencies. Keep the original polymorphic API while compiling the upstream React 18-era generic JSX against React 19 types.

modifications.change: Ported the upstream row state machine to strict TypeScript and added cancellation cleanup, semantic keyboard reveal and focus handling, logical RTL sides, resize remeasurement, a single frame scheduler, and a destructive-result announcement.
modifications.reason: Clear the pinned source's touch, keyboard, focus, RTL, responsive measurement, lifecycle, and perceivable-result conditional gates without replacing its drag or action state machine.

modifications.change: Added logical RTL geometry, the semantic reveal route and focus styles, pan-y mobile behavior, and static reduced-motion outcomes to the upstream row CSS.
modifications.reason: Keep leading and trailing behavior correct across direction, coarse pointers, keyboard use, and reduced-motion preferences.

modifications.change: Ported the action to strict TypeScript, made button the semantic default, added a keyboard fallback for custom tags, gated tab focus by reveal state, and tracked every delayed callback. Added a local render-component type bridge for React 19 polymorphic JSX without changing runtime behavior.
modifications.reason: Remove click-only generic actions, hidden focus targets, undeclared runtime dependencies, and callbacks that could fire after unmount. Keep the original polymorphic API while compiling the upstream React 18-era generic JSX against React 19 types.

modifications.change: Adapted the upstream action skin for a native button with accessible target and focus sizing and a reduced-motion width branch.
modifications.reason: Preserve caller-owned styling while keeping the semantic action usable on keyboard, touch, and reduced-motion setups.

modifications.change: Mechanically ported the upstream leading-action child helper to strict TypeScript.
modifications.reason: Retain the complete upstream helper closure without PropTypes.

modifications.change: Mechanically ported the upstream trailing-action child helper to strict TypeScript.
modifications.reason: Retain the complete upstream helper closure without PropTypes.

modifications.change: Updated the upstream declarations for the strict ports, semantic action default, and reveal-label extension.
modifications.reason: Keep the copied declaration closure consistent with the preserved runtime exports and narrow accessibility additions.
